decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.27°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution and reduce the thermal hotspot. I will increase the spreader conductivity by a factor of 1.5, which is within the allowed single-step ratio [0.5x, 2.0x] and bounds [20.0, 500.0].

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the local temperature gradient above the chip and lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though this is a simulation.
